title: Cool Questions — does US citizenship extend beyond Earth's atmosphere?
html:
TL;DR

  • One-question Apple Note from July 5, 2023: "Does US citizenship extend beyond the Earth's atmosphere? Will you be an American on Mars?" — opened as a "COOL QUESTIONS:" list, only one ever added.
  • Sibling note to same-day startup advice AGM capture — Yahir was in brainstorm-and-collect mode that morning, batching unrelated micro-thoughts into separate notes.
  • The question itself is a jurisdiction-meets-space-frontier curiosity — adjacent to surveillance state / sovereignty-of-data themes the vault already touches, and to the broader disruption / frontier-tech leanings in the May 2023 ideas dump.
  • Title format ("COOL QUESTIONS:") is a list-template Yahir opened and abandoned — a recurring failure mode where a "container" note holds one item and dies.
  • Practical answer (offered for graph completeness): yes, US citizenship is personal and travels with the person, not bound to terrestrial jurisdiction; but legal jurisdiction on Mars is open under the Outer Space Treaty — so Yahir's instinct that there's an interesting gap here is correct.

Why this micro-note is worth a node

  • It's a rare on-the-nose civic-question artifact in a corpus heavy on personal/spiritual material — adds a small civic/sovereignty cluster the graph mostly lacks.
  • Pairs interestingly with the same-day AGM startup-advice note — Yahir's morning was equal parts "how do I build" and "how does the world even work". Both modes feeding the same builder energy.

Connections

  • 2023 07 05 startup advice agm — same-day sibling note
  • — abandoned-list-template subtype
  • disruption · surveillance state — adjacent civic-frontier concepts
  • piercing prompts — provocative-question format
  • antonio garcia martinez — same-day intellectual diet

related.label: Startup advice — Antonio García Martínez two-traits + monomaniacal focus
